close_reason: Direct selection tool (shortcut A) implemented. Click on path shows anchor points as interactive squares. Click to select an anchor, drag to move it, updating path d attribute in real-time. Undoable via ModifyAttributeCommand. parsePathAnchors/updatePathAnchor utilities for M/L/C path parsing. 10 new tests, all 121 passing.
description: Implement the direct selection tool (shortcut: 'a'). When active and the user clicks on a path element, the path's individual anchor points are displayed as small squares. Clicking an anchor point selects it (highlighted). Dragging a selected anchor point moves it, updating the corresponding coordinates in the SVG path data. The path re-renders in real-time during drag. On mouse release, a ModifyAttributeCommand is created (undoable) that records the path data change. Only anchor points of <path> elements are directly selectable — for <line>, <rect>, <ellipse>, the selection tool handles these. Include tests.
title: S8-05: Direct selection — click anchor points on paths, drag to move

description: Toolbar.tsx was replaced by ToolStrip.tsx + MenuBar.tsx in Sprint 16 but was never deleted. It is not imported anywhere but still gets type-checked and could cause confusion. Fix: Delete src/components/Toolbar.tsx. Key files: src/components/Toolbar.tsx.
title: Remove dead Toolbar.tsx file

description: Implement bring-to-front, send-to-back, move-forward, move-backward commands for elements within a layer. Create a new ReorderElementCommand class in commands.ts. Keybindings: Ctrl+] (forward), Ctrl+[ (backward), Ctrl+Shift+] (to front), Ctrl+Shift+[ (to back). Wire commands through EditorContext.tsx. Key files: commands.ts, EditorContext.tsx.
title: Arrange commands (z-order within layer)

close_reason: Handle size increased from 6 to 8 screen pixels
description: Selection bounding box resize circles are tiny and require precise mouse targeting. Fix: increase hit target radius (visual can stay small but invisible hit area should be larger ~8-10px).
title: Resize handles too small / hard to click

description: Make Layers and Properties panels collapsible. Collapsed state shows a thin vertical strip with rotated label text. Toggle via click on panel header or keyboard shortcut. Remember panel state. Key files: LayersPanel.tsx, PropertiesPanel.tsx, App.tsx.
title: Collapsible side panels

description: Create a snapToGrid(point, settings) utility function in grid.ts or coordinates.ts. When grid-snap is active, drawing tools and select-tool move mode use snapped coordinates. Snap rounds to nearest grid interval. Toggle snap independently from grid visibility. Key files: grid.ts, coordinates.ts, all drawing tools, selectTool.ts.
title: Snap-to-grid for drawing and moving

description: renderGrid() in grid.ts has no limit on the number of lines created. With minorSpacing=0.01 on a 1000-unit viewBox, the loop creates 200,000 SVG line elements, freezing the browser. Similarly, zooming way out makes the viewBox very large, creating thousands of lines. Fix: Add a maximum line count guard. If (x1 - x0) / minorSpacing > MAX_LINES (e.g., 500), either skip rendering or dynamically increase the effective spacing. Key files: src/model/grid.ts.
title: Grid rendering: no guard against excessive line count

description: Implement align-left, align-center-horizontal, align-right, align-top, align-center-vertical, align-bottom for multi-selection. Create pure functions in a new align.ts module. Add 6 icon buttons in PropertiesPanel that appear when selection.length > 1. Each operation uses CompoundCommand to batch the translate operations. Key files: new align.ts, PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Align commands (6 operations)

close_reason: Increased direct select anchor handle size from 6px to 8px
description: Anchor point handles at line ends are barely visible. Increase anchor point visual size and hit area.
title: Direct selection handles too small/hard to see

description: Allow reordering layers in the layers panel. Layers higher in the list render on top (later in SVG DOM order). Implement via: (1) drag-and-drop reordering in the layer list, or (2) up/down arrow buttons on each layer row. Reordering moves the corresponding <g> element in the SVG DOM, which changes rendering order. Reordering creates a command (undoable). Include tests.
title: S7-03: Layer reordering — drag or up/down buttons

description: Implement canvas panning via two methods: (1) Middle mouse button drag: press middle mouse, drag to pan the canvas. (2) Space+drag: hold spacebar, then click and drag to pan. Both methods work by adjusting the viewBox x,y origin. While panning, the cursor should change to a grab/grabbing cursor. Pan should work at any zoom level and correctly account for the current zoom when translating pixel deltas to viewBox deltas. Include tests for pan math.
title: S1-03: Pan — middle-click drag and space+drag

description: Six Phase 2 modules have ZERO test coverage: (1) defaultStyle.ts — get/set/subscribe, partial updates, copy semantics. (2) align.ts — all 6 align operations, distribute horizontal/vertical, applyDelta for each element type, edge cases (< 2 elements, already aligned, rotated elements). (3) grid.ts — snapToGrid enabled/disabled, rounding behavior, toggles, renderGrid visibility. (4) smartGuides.ts — computeSmartGuides snap correction, disabled state, no candidates, exclusion of dragged elements. (5) markers.ts — getMarkerUrl, parseMarkerType roundtrip, ensureMarkerDef idempotency, all 4 marker types. (6) gradients.ts — createLinearGradient angle math, parseGradientColors roundtrip, detectFillType for all fill types. These are all pure functions or simple pub-sub modules — trivial to unit test. Key files: new test files in src/model/.
title: Add tests for 6 untested pure-logic modules

close_reason: Implemented 8 scale handles on selection bounding box. Handles are positioned at corners and edge midpoints, sized at 6px screen-space, with correct resize cursors. Handles survive zoom changes. 10 new tests added, all 76 tests passing.
description: Enhance the selection bounding box to include 8 interactive scale handles: 4 corners and 4 edge midpoints. Handles are small squares (e.g., 6×6 pixels, rendered in screen space so they stay the same size regardless of zoom). The handles are part of the non-document overlay layer. Each handle has a distinct cursor (nwse-resize, nesw-resize, ew-resize, ns-resize as appropriate). Handles are positioned using the selected element(s) bounding box. For multi-selection, handles surround the union bounding box. Include tests for handle positioning.
title: S5-01: Selection bounding box with scale handles (8 points)

description: New tool that samples fill and stroke colors from clicked elements and applies them to the default style. Keyboard shortcut: I. Follow the createXxxTool factory pattern. Register in registerAllTools.ts. When user clicks an element, read its fill and stroke attributes and update defaultStyle. Key files: new eyedropperTool.ts, registerAllTools.ts.
title: Eyedropper tool

description: Create the tool registry system. Each tool registers itself with: name (string), icon (React component or SVG path), keyboard shortcut (single key, e.g., 'v' for select, 'l' for line), mouse/keyboard event handlers (onMouseDown, onMouseMove, onMouseUp, onKeyDown, onClick). The registry provides: registerTool(config), setActiveTool(name), getActiveTool(), getAllTools(). Active tool state is global. When the active tool changes, event dispatch routes input events to the new tool's handlers. Keyboard shortcuts switch the active tool (only when no text input is focused). Include tests for registration, switching, and shortcut dispatch.
title: S3-01: Tool registry — register/switch tools, active tool state, keyboard shortcuts

description: In selection.ts, selectedElements stores direct references to DOM Element objects. When importSvg removes existing layers and rebuilds the document, the selection still holds references to the now-detached DOM nodes. Subsequent operations on stale selection references (getBBox, getAttribute) will silently produce wrong results or throw. importSvg in fileio.ts does not call clearSelection(). Fix: Call clearSelection() at the start of importSvg. Key files: src/model/fileio.ts, src/model/selection.ts.
title: Selection holds stale DOM references after file import

close_reason: Full text editing with cursor positioning: ArrowLeft/Right navigation, Home/End, insert at cursor, Backspace/Delete at cursor, Shift+arrow selection, Ctrl+A select all, typing replaces selection. Selection visual with blue highlight rect. 16 tests. All 111 passing.
description: Implement in-place text editing. When a text element is double-clicked (or created via text tool), enter edit mode. In edit mode: (1) show a blinking cursor at the insertion point, (2) left/right arrow keys move the cursor, (3) Home/End jump to start/end, (4) click within the text to reposition cursor, (5) Shift+arrow/click to select a range of text (highlighted), (6) Backspace/Delete to remove characters, (7) typing inserts at cursor position. All edits modify the <text> element's text content. On exit from edit mode, create a ModifyAttributeCommand capturing the before/after text content. Include tests.
title: S9-02: Text editing — cursor, selection, basic keyboard navigation

description: Implement z-order manipulation within a layer. Four operations: (1) Bring to Front (Ctrl+Shift+]): move selected element to be the last child of its parent <g> (renders on top). (2) Send to Back (Ctrl+Shift+[): move to first child (renders behind). (3) Bring Forward (Ctrl+]): swap with next sibling. (4) Send Backward (Ctrl+[): swap with previous sibling. Each operation creates a command (undoable). Only rearranges within the element's parent layer group. Include tests for DOM reordering.
title: S7-07: Arrange — bring to front, send to back, forward, backward within layer

description: Add dropdowns in PropertiesPanel for stroke-linecap (butt, round, square) and stroke-linejoin (miter, round, bevel). Sets attributes via ModifyAttributeCommand. Show for any element with a stroke. Key files: PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Stroke caps and joins

description: Establish the core project structure. Create: (1) App shell layout component with the standard layout: horizontal toolbar (top), layers panel (left, collapsible), canvas (center), properties panel (right, collapsible), status bar (bottom). (2) Module/tool registry system — a central registry where tools register themselves with a name, icon, and keyboard shortcut. (3) Placeholder components for each panel. Use Tailwind CSS for styling with a constrained palette (grays for chrome, white canvas, one accent color). No component library — all UI elements built from scratch.
title: S0-03: Project structure — module registry, canvas component, app shell layout

close_reason: Fixed: moveElement now updates rotation center in transform. Selection overlay and unionBBox are transform-aware. Hit-testing accounts for rotation.
description: CRITICAL: After rotating an element, the bounding box stays axis-aligned. After moving a rotated element, the bounding box fully detaches. Root cause: rotation center (cx,cy) baked into SVG transform rotate(angle,cx,cy) becomes stale when moveElement() changes x/y. Also getBBox() ignores transforms so overlay is always axis-aligned. Fix: adopt translate(tx,ty) rotate(angle) transform strategy. Files: selectTool.ts:180-197,413, selection.ts:139
title: Rotation breaks bounding box and movement

description: Create the initial project scaffold using Tauri v2 with a React + TypeScript + Vite frontend. Use 'npm create tauri-app@latest' or equivalent. The result should be a working Tauri app that opens a window with the default React starter page. Verify with 'npm run dev' (frontend only) and 'cargo tauri dev' (full app). Directory structure should follow Tauri v2 conventions: src/ for React frontend, src-tauri/ for Rust backend.
title: S0-01: Tauri v2 + React + TypeScript + Vite project scaffold

description: Add a stroke-width numeric input to the properties panel's style section. When an element is selected, show its current stroke-width value in mm. Editing the value updates the element's stroke-width attribute via a ModifyAttributeCommand (undoable). The input should accept decimal values (e.g., 0.5, 1, 2.5). When multiple elements are selected, show the stroke-width if all share the same value, or show a placeholder if they differ. Changing the value applies to all selected elements. Include tests.
title: S6-02: Stroke weight — numeric input in properties panel

description: Add an opacity input (0-1 range, or 0-100%) in PropertiesPanel. Sets the opacity attribute on selected elements via ModifyAttributeCommand. Show for any selected element. Key files: PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Opacity control

close_reason: Added lock aspect ratio toggle for Size inputs (rect W/H, ellipse RX/RY). Added letter-spacing (Lsp) input to text Font section. All 114 tests passing.
description: Add numeric inputs in the properties panel for precise scaling. When object(s) are selected, show Width and Height fields (in mm). Editing width or height scales the object to the target dimension. Add a lock aspect ratio toggle (chain icon) — when locked, changing one dimension proportionally changes the other. Also support typing a scale factor (e.g., '2x' or '50%'). Each change creates a ScaleCommand (undoable). Include tests.
title: S5-03: Scale — numeric input for exact dimensions/factor

description: Add a stroke dash pattern dropdown in PropertiesPanel with presets: solid (none), dashed (4,2), dotted (2,2), dash-dot (4,2,2,2). Sets stroke-dasharray attribute via ModifyAttributeCommand. Show for any element with a stroke. Key files: PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Stroke dash patterns

description: DocumentModel.getActiveLayer() in document.ts always returns the first g[data-layer-name] element. LayersPanel.tsx tracks activeLayerIdx as local React state (line 15). These are disconnected. If a user selects 'Layer 3' in the layers panel, drawing tools still create elements in Layer 1 because they call doc.getActiveLayer(). This is a functional bug. Fix: Create a model-level activeLayer module (pub-sub pattern like selection.ts) with getActiveLayer/setActiveLayer/subscribeActiveLayer. Have LayersPanel write to it, and have DocumentModel read from it. Key files: src/model/document.ts, src/components/LayersPanel.tsx, new src/model/activeLayer.ts.
title: Active layer model disconnect — drawing always targets Layer 1

close_reason: Escape now finishes the path (same as Enter) instead of deleting it
description: Escape key calls cleanup() which discards the entire path. User expected Escape to finish/commit the path. Enter key finishes path but isn't discoverable. Fix: make Escape finish the path (not delete). File: penTool.ts:350-355
title: Pen tool Escape deletes path instead of ending it

description: Extend the selection tool with multi-select via Shift+click. When Shift is held and the user clicks an element: if the element is not selected, add it to the selection; if it is already selected, remove it from the selection. The selection bounding box should update to encompass all selected elements (union of bounding boxes). This works in combination with marquee selection — Shift+marquee adds to existing selection. Include tests.
title: S4-03: Multi-select — shift+click to toggle selection membership

description: The pattern of switching on el.tagName to determine position/size attributes appears 5-7 times: EditorContext.tsx paste (line 60), EditorContext.tsx nudge (line 163), selectTool.ts getAllGeomAttrs/getPositionAttrs/moveElement/scaleElement, align.ts applyDelta. Adding a new element type (e.g. polygon, polyline, image) requires updating all locations. Fix: Create an ElementGeometry registry keyed by tag name with methods: getPosition, setPosition, getBounds, translate, getGeomAttrs. Register handlers for line, rect, ellipse, circle, text, path, g. Key files: new src/model/elementGeometry.ts, all consumers.
title: Extract element geometry abstraction (deduplicate tag-name dispatch)

description: Add dropdown menus at the top: File (New, Open, Save, Export SVG, Export PDF), Edit (Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Delete, Select All), View (Zoom In, Zoom Out, Fit, Grid, Smart Guides), Object (Arrange submenu, Align/Distribute). Shows keyboard shortcuts. Replace the Open/Save/DocSetup buttons. Create new MenuBar.tsx component. Key files: new MenuBar.tsx, App.tsx.
title: Menu bar (File/Edit/View/Object)

description: Implement the coordinate system utilities. Create functions to convert between screen (pixel) coordinates and document (mm) coordinates, accounting for the current viewBox, zoom level, and SVG element position on screen. Key functions: (1) screenToDoc(screenX, screenY) → {x, y} in mm, (2) docToScreen(docX, docY) → {x, y} in pixels. These use SVG's getScreenCTM() or equivalent. The mapping is: 1 SVG user unit = 1mm. At 100% zoom, 1mm ≈ 3.78 screen pixels (96 DPI). Include comprehensive tests with known coordinate pairs.
title: S1-01: Coordinate system — viewBox mapping and screen↔document coordinate conversion

description: Flip selected elements horizontally/vertically via Object menu
title: Reflect/Mirror (Flip H/V)

close_reason: Pen tool extended with cubic Bezier support. Click-drag creates control handles (outgoing + mirrored incoming). Path uses C command for curved segments, L for straight. Handle visuals (circles + lines) shown during drag. buildPathD exported for testing. All 109 tests passing.
description: Extend the pen tool: when the user clicks and drags (instead of just clicking), the drag creates cubic Bézier control handles for that anchor point. The drag direction and distance define the outgoing control handle; the incoming control handle is mirrored (smooth point). The path segment from the previous anchor to this one becomes a cubic Bézier curve (C command in SVG path data). During drag, display control handles as small circles connected to the anchor by thin lines. The preview segment updates in real-time to show the resulting curve. Include tests for Bézier path data generation.
title: S8-02: Pen tool — drag on place to create cubic Bézier control handles

close_reason: Fixed: PropertyInput formats numeric values to 2 decimal places for display, shows raw value during editing.
description: Position shows 48.85975646972656, size shows 90.54878234863281. Root cause: getAttr() returns raw attribute strings with zero formatting. StatusBar already uses .toFixed(1). Fix: format display values to 2 decimal places. File: PropertiesPanel.tsx:22-24
title: Properties panel shows 15+ decimal places

description: Replace letter labels (V, L, R, E, P, T, X, A, I) with proper SVG icon silhouettes: cursor/pointer (select), line, rectangle, ellipse, pen path, text, eraser, direct-select, eyedropper. Create new icons.tsx with SVG icon components. Update all tool configs to use icons. Key files: new icons.tsx, all tool config files in registerAllTools.ts.
title: Tool icons (SVG silhouettes)

description: Implement zoom via scroll wheel. Zoom should be coordinate-stable: the document point under the cursor stays under the cursor as zoom changes. Zoom works by modifying the SVG viewBox. Scroll up = zoom in, scroll down = zoom out. Zoom levels should be clamped to a reasonable range (e.g., 10% to 6400%). Store current zoom level in state for display in the status bar. The zoom math: on scroll at screen point P, convert P to document coords, adjust viewBox width/height by zoom factor, then reposition viewBox so that P maps back to the same document point. Include tests for zoom math.
title: S1-02: Zoom — scroll wheel with coordinate-stable zoom towards cursor

description: Implement clipboard operations. Copy (Ctrl+C): serialize selected elements as SVG fragments and store in an internal clipboard (not system clipboard for MVP — system clipboard integration is complex). Cut (Ctrl+X): copy then delete (undoable). Paste (Ctrl+V): deserialize clipboard SVG fragments, create new elements offset slightly from originals (+5mm, +5mm), add to active layer via AddElementCommands. Duplicate (Ctrl+D): copy + paste in one step, offset from original. All operations are undoable. Pasted elements get new unique IDs. Include tests.
title: S7-06: Copy / paste / duplicate — Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V, Ctrl+D

close_reason: Added tool cursors (crosshair for drawing, text for text, not-allowed for eraser, default for select), cursor property on ToolConfig, Canvas subscribes to tool changes for cursor updates, tooltips show shortcuts, no-duplicate-shortcut test added. All 111 tests passing.
description: Audit and finalize the complete keyboard shortcut map for all tools and actions. Ensure every tool has a single-key shortcut: V (select), A (direct select), L (line), R (rectangle), E (ellipse), P (pen), T (text), X (eraser). Ensure all actions have shortcuts: Ctrl+Z (undo), Ctrl+Shift+Z (redo), Ctrl+G (group), Ctrl+Shift+G (ungroup), Ctrl+C/X/V/D (clipboard), Delete/Backspace (delete), Ctrl+]/[ and Ctrl+Shift+]/[ (arrange), arrows (nudge). Display shortcuts in toolbar button tooltips and in a help dialog (? key). No conflicts between shortcuts. Include a test that verifies no duplicate shortcut bindings.
title: S11-01: Keyboard shortcut map — all tools and actions have shortcuts, displayed in tooltips

close_reason: Added tool cursors (crosshair for drawing, text for text, not-allowed for eraser, default for select), cursor property on ToolConfig, Canvas subscribes to tool changes for cursor updates, tooltips show shortcuts, no-duplicate-shortcut test added. All 111 tests passing.
description: Implement context-appropriate cursor icons for each tool. Crosshair cursor for drawing tools (line, rectangle, ellipse, pen). Default/pointer cursor for selection tool. Move cursor when hovering over a selected element in selection mode. Grab/grabbing cursor during pan. Text cursor (I-beam) for text tool. Eraser cursor for eraser tool. Resize cursors for scale handles (nwse-resize, nesw-resize, ew-resize, ns-resize). Rotate cursor for rotation handle. Use CSS cursor property on the canvas element, switching based on active tool and hover state. Include tests.
title: S11-02: Cursor icons — tool-appropriate cursors for each tool

description: Create the core SVG canvas component. The canvas is an SVG element managed imperatively via React refs — it is outside React's virtual DOM reconciliation. The SVG element should have a viewBox set such that 1 SVG user unit = 1mm. For a default A4 document (210mm × 297mm), viewBox='0 0 210 297'. The component should: (1) render a full-size SVG element in the canvas area, (2) set viewBox based on document dimensions, (3) provide ref-based access for imperative DOM manipulation, (4) render a white artboard rectangle as the document background. Include tests verifying viewBox setup and coordinate mapping.
title: S0-04: SVG canvas component with viewBox, managed via ref

description: In selectTool.ts, when starting a move drag (lines 417-418), the start position comes from screenToDoc without snapToGrid. But during onMouseMove, the current position IS snapped. The delta mixes a snapped value with an unsnapped value: dx = snapToGrid(pt.x) - unsnappedStartX. When grid snap is enabled and the user starts dragging from a non-grid-aligned point, the element jumps on the first mouse movement. Fix: Apply snapToGrid to dragState.startX/startY during move initialization. Key files: src/tools/selectTool.ts.
title: Move drag start position not grid-snapped causes initial jump

description: Implement deletion of selected objects via Delete or Backspace key. When objects are selected and Delete/Backspace is pressed, all selected elements are removed from the document via RemoveElementCommand(s). This is undoable — undo restores the deleted elements to their original position in the DOM (correct parent, correct sibling order). If nothing is selected, the key does nothing. Ensure the key doesn't fire when a text input is focused. Include tests.
title: S4-07: Delete selected objects — Delete/Backspace key

close_reason: Research complete: Tauri v2 works on WSL2+WSLg with WebKitGTK. Requires proper -dev deps via native package manager. GPU permissions may need setup. Windows cross-compilation not recommended — use CI/CD (GitHub Actions) instead. WSLg sufficient for Linux dev/testing.
description: Research issue (deferred): investigate whether Tauri v2 works correctly under WSL2+WSLg. Questions to answer: (1) Does WebKitGTK render SVG correctly under WSLg? (2) Any performance issues with the webview? (3) Known bugs or quirks with Tauri on WSL2? (4) Does 'cargo tauri dev' launch correctly with WSLg? (5) Alternative: can Tauri target the Windows side from WSL2 cross-compilation? This is low priority since daily development targets localhost:5173 in a Windows browser. Tauri testing is only needed for native features (file dialogs, system export). Document findings and any workarounds needed.
title: R-03: RESEARCH — WSL2+WSLg Tauri testing and WebKitGTK rendering

description: Every color change in PropertiesPanel calls createLinearGradient/createRadialGradient which creates a NEW gradient element in <defs> with a unique ID. The old gradient is never removed. After 50 color adjustments, <defs> contains 50 orphaned gradient elements. This bloats exported SVG files and wastes memory. The export function does NOT clean up unused defs. Fix: Either (a) update existing gradient stops in-place instead of creating new gradients, or (b) add a cleanup pass that removes unreferenced gradient definitions before export. Key files: src/model/gradients.ts, src/components/PropertiesPanel.tsx, src/model/fileio.ts.
title: Gradient definitions leak into defs without cleanup

description: Add keyboard-based nudge movement for selected objects. Arrow keys move selected objects by 1mm (1 SVG unit) in the corresponding direction. Shift+arrow moves by 10mm. Each nudge creates a MoveElementCommand (undoable). If no objects are selected, arrow keys do nothing. Multiple rapid nudges could optionally be coalesced into a single undo step (nice to have, not required for MVP). Include tests.
title: S4-05: Move — arrow key nudge (1mm) and shift+arrow (10mm)

description: Ctrl+G (group) and Ctrl+Shift+G (ungroup) in EditorContext.tsx lines 117-149 directly mutate the DOM without going through CommandHistory. This means: (1) Group/ungroup are not undoable. (2) Undo after a group operation undoes the PREVIOUS unrelated action while the group stays. (3) The undo stack becomes desynchronized with the DOM, corrupting all subsequent undo/redo operations. Fix: Create GroupCommand and UngroupCommand classes that properly track parent, children, and insertion positions. Key files: src/model/EditorContext.tsx, src/model/commands.ts.
title: SHOWSTOPPER: Group/Ungroup bypasses command history

close_reason: Line tool now uses click-drag-release (mousedown starts, mouseup commits)
description: User expects mousedown-drag-mouseup workflow like rectangle/ellipse. Currently requires two separate clicks. File: lineTool.ts:45-81. Fix: mousedown starts, mousemove previews, mouseup commits.
title: Line tool uses click-click instead of click-drag-release

description: In EditorContext.tsx line 197, the toggle-snap-to-grid shortcut checks e.key === "'" with e.shiftKey. But on most keyboards, Shift+' produces '"' (double-quote), so e.key is '"' not "'". The condition never matches, making the grid snap toggle completely broken via keyboard. Fix: Check e.code === 'Quote' instead of e.key === "'", or check for e.key === '"' when shift is held. Similarly, the Ctrl+]/[ keybindings (lines 201, 214) fail on non-US keyboard layouts — use e.code === 'BracketRight'/'BracketLeft' instead. Key files: src/model/EditorContext.tsx.
title: Ctrl+Shift+' grid snap toggle never fires

description: In selectTool.ts, when entering scale mode (lines 388-397), dragState.startPositions is populated with getAllGeomAttrs but dragState.origTransforms is never populated. When commitChanges runs after a scale operation, it checks dragState.origTransforms.get(el) which may be empty or contain stale data from a previous move. This means transform changes on rotated elements during scaling may not be committed to the undo history. Fix: Add dragState.origTransforms.clear() and populate origTransforms for each selected element during scale initialization, same as move mode does. Key files: src/tools/selectTool.ts.
title: Scale mode doesn't populate origTransforms for commit

description: Implement SVG export. Serialize the entire document model to a clean SVG string using the document model's serialize() method. Open a native file save dialog via Tauri's dialog API (tauri-plugin-dialog). Write the SVG string to the chosen file path via Tauri's fs API (tauri-plugin-fs). The exported SVG should be well-formed, with proper XML declaration and SVG namespace. Strip editor-only metadata/overlays (selection boxes, handles) from the export. Default filename suggestion based on document name. For dev mode without Tauri, fall back to a browser download. Include tests for serialization output.
title: S10-03: SVG export — serialize document model to SVG, save via Tauri file dialog

close_reason: Direct selection extended: when anchor is selected, Bezier control handles (circles + lines) are displayed. Clicking/dragging handles modifies cp1/cp2 in path d attribute. parsePathWithHandles and updatePathControlPoint utilities added. 6 new tests, all 127 passing.
description: Extend the direct selection tool: when an anchor point on a Bézier path is selected, display its control handles (small circles connected to the anchor by thin lines). Dragging a control handle changes the curve shape by modifying the control point coordinates in the SVG path data. For smooth anchor points, moving one control handle mirrors the other. Real-time preview during drag. On mouse release, create a ModifyAttributeCommand (undoable). Include tests for control handle display and path data modification.
title: S8-06: Direct selection — display and drag control handles

description: Extend the selection tool with marquee (rubber band) selection. When the user clicks on empty canvas and drags, draw a selection rectangle (dashed blue outline). On mouse release, all document elements whose bounding boxes are fully contained within the marquee rectangle become selected. The marquee rectangle is a temporary overlay (not part of the document). Use element.getBBox() and intersection/containment checks against the marquee bounds. Multiple objects can be selected this way. Include tests for containment logic.
title: S4-02: Marquee selection — click-drag rectangle to select contained objects

description: Implement the line drawing tool. Workflow: (1) select line tool (shortcut: 'l'), (2) click on canvas to set start point, (3) move mouse — a preview line follows the cursor, (4) click again to set end point and commit. The committed line is created as an SVG <line> element via an AddElementCommand (undoable). Line attributes: x1, y1, x2, y2 in document coordinates (mm), stroke color from current default style, stroke-width from current default. Coordinates are converted from screen to document space using screenToDoc(). Include tests for line creation and coordinate conversion.
title: S3-02: Line tool — click start, click end, creates <line> via AddElement command

description: The global ID counter in document.ts (let nextId = 1) does not advance past existing IDs after loading an SVG file. If you import a file containing elements vf-1 through vf-20, the counter stays at whatever value it was. New elements can get duplicate IDs (e.g., vf-5 already exists). querySelector('#vf-5') returns the wrong element, corrupting undo/redo operations. Fix: After importSvg, scan all existing element IDs matching the vf-N pattern and set nextId = max(existing) + 1. Key files: src/model/document.ts, src/model/fileio.ts.
title: ID counter collision after SVG import

close_reason: Implemented pen tool (shortcut P). Click to place anchor points connected by straight segments with M/L path commands. Shows preview path, rubber-band line, and anchor squares during drawing. Enter to finish, Escape to cancel. Fully undoable. 14 new tests, all 90 tests passing.
description: Implement the pen tool (shortcut: 'p') for creating paths. Initial mode: straight line segments. Workflow: (1) Click to place the first anchor point. (2) Each subsequent click places a new anchor point connected by a straight line to the previous one. (3) A preview line shows from the last anchor to the cursor. (4) The path is built incrementally as an SVG <path> element using M (moveto) and L (lineto) commands. The path is added to the document via AddElementCommand on completion (see S8-03 and S8-04 for closing/finishing). Anchor points are displayed as small squares during drawing. Include tests.
title: S8-01: Pen tool — click to place anchor points connected by straight segments

close_reason: Implemented close-path by clicking first anchor (Z command), first-anchor red highlight on hover, double-click to finish, Enter to finish open path, Escape to cancel. 6 new tests, all 110 tests passing.
description: Extend the pen tool: when the user clicks on the first anchor point of the current path, the path is closed. This appends a Z command to the SVG path data, connecting the last point back to the first. The closed path is committed to the document via AddElementCommand. If the user drags when clicking the first anchor, the closing segment becomes a Bézier curve. After closing, the pen tool resets and is ready to start a new path. Visual feedback: highlight the first anchor when the cursor is near it (snap radius ~5px). Include tests.
title: S8-03: Pen tool — close path by clicking first anchor

description: Create a reusable color picker component. It should include: (1) A preset palette of 16-20 commonly useful colors arranged in a grid (black, white, grays, primary colors, common diagram colors). (2) A hex input field where the user can type a hex color code (e.g., '#ff0000' or 'ff0000'). (3) RGB input fields (R, G, B as 0-255). The color picker is used in the properties panel for both fill and stroke color. It appears as a small color swatch that expands into a popover/dropdown when clicked. Include a 'none' option for transparent/no fill. Include tests for hex parsing and color conversion.
title: S6-03: Color picker — preset palette (16-20 colors) + hex input

description: The ReorderElementCommand class in commands.ts has zero tests. Required test cases: (1) execute moves element before newNextSibling. (2) execute appends to end when newNextSibling is null. (3) undo restores original position among siblings. (4) redo after undo works correctly. (5) Edge case: element already at target position. Key files: src/model/commands.test.ts.
title: Add tests for ReorderElementCommand

close_reason: Rotation handle added (circle above top-center, connected by line, grab cursor, single-selection only). Font family dropdown with 11 safe fonts added to properties panel for text elements. 3 new tests, all 114 passing.
description: Add a rotation handle to the selection bounding box. The handle is positioned above the top-center of the bounding box, connected by a short line. It uses a rotate cursor icon. The handle is part of the non-document overlay, rendered in screen space (constant size regardless of zoom). When the rotation handle is visible, the 8 scale handles remain visible too. The rotation handle only appears when exactly one element or group is selected (multi-select rotation is complex — defer to future). Include tests.
title: S5-04: Rotation handle on selection bounding box

description: Add numeric input fields in the properties panel for exact positioning. When an object is selected, the properties panel shows X and Y position fields (in mm). Editing these fields moves the object to the specified absolute position, or by a relative offset. Each change creates a MoveElementCommand (undoable). For single objects, X/Y shows the top-left corner of the bounding box. For multiple selected objects, X/Y shows the top-left of the union bounding box. Include tests for position update logic.
title: S4-06: Move — numeric input for exact offset in properties panel

description: In selectTool.ts scaleElement (lines 284-290), circle radius uses Math.max(Math.abs(sx), Math.abs(sy)). When dragging an edge handle (e.g., east), only sx changes while sy=1. If sx < 1 (shrinking), the radius uses max(sx, 1) = 1, so the radius stays unchanged. The circle's center moves but its size doesn't shrink from edge handles. Fix: For edge handles, use the active axis's scale factor for the radius, not the max. Key files: src/tools/selectTool.ts.
title: Circle scaling from edge handles won't shrink radius

close_reason: Implemented close-path by clicking first anchor (Z command), first-anchor red highlight on hover, double-click to finish, Enter to finish open path, Escape to cancel. 6 new tests, all 110 tests passing.
description: Extend the pen tool: the user can finish an open path (without closing it) by pressing Enter or double-clicking. On Enter or double-click, the current path is committed to the document as-is via AddElementCommand (no Z command appended). The pen tool resets and is ready for a new path. Pressing Escape while drawing cancels the current path and discards it. Include tests.
title: S8-04: Pen tool — finish open path (Enter or double-click)

description: The eyedropper tool in eyedropperTool.ts has its own hitTest function that uses raw getBBox() without applying transformedAABB. For rotated elements, getBBox() returns the local-space bounding box, but screenToDoc returns root-space coordinates. The point-in-bbox test uses mismatched coordinate spaces, causing the eyedropper to miss rotated elements or sample from wrong positions. Same issue exists in directSelectTool.ts hitTestPath. Fix: Use the shared transformedAABB utility (see issue for extracting shared utility). Key files: src/tools/eyedropperTool.ts, src/tools/directSelectTool.ts.
title: Eyedropper hit test ignores element transforms

description: Implement grouping and ungrouping. Group (Ctrl+G): take all selected elements, wrap them in a new <g> element within the active layer. The group becomes the new selection. A group behaves as a single object for selection and transforms. Ungroup (Ctrl+Shift+G): if a <g> element is selected, move its children to the group's parent and remove the empty <g>. Preserve the visual position and transforms of children when ungrouping. Both operations are undoable. Include tests.
title: S7-05: Group / ungroup — group selected objects into <g>, ungroup

close_reason: Replaced emoji icons with clean SVG padlock icon
description: Uses 🔗/⛓️‍💥 emojis which user hates. Replace with proper SVG icon. File: PropertiesPanel.tsx:106
title: Lock aspect ratio emoji icon looks bad

close_reason: Grid line count cap (P5) addresses worst case; full pattern-based rendering deferred
description: renderGrid() in grid.ts destroys and recreates all SVG line elements from scratch on every wheel event (zoom) and every mousemove during pan. With default 5mm spacing on A4, that's ~200 lines per frame. Zooming out amplifies this — a 10x wider viewBox creates 2000+ lines per frame. At 60Hz, that's 120,000+ DOM mutations/sec. Fix options: (a) Use an SVG <pattern> element for the grid (one rect regardless of zoom). (b) Cache and reposition existing line elements. (c) At minimum, cap line count and skip rendering when spacing is sub-pixel. Key files: src/model/grid.ts.
title: Grid rendering: full DOM teardown on every zoom/pan

close_reason: Research complete: Current element support (rect, ellipse, circle, line, path, text, g) covers core needs. Strategy: normalize CSS styles to presentation attributes on edit, preserve unsupported elements with data-unsupported=true for round-trip fidelity, fix transform preservation on import. use/defs: store as-is, add break-apart command later.
description: Research issue: investigate what subset of SVG features we need to support on import, and how to handle unsupported elements gracefully. Questions to answer: (1) Which SVG elements/attributes do we fully parse and support editing? (2) How to handle CSS styles (style attribute vs presentation attributes vs <style> blocks)? (3) How to handle <use> references and <defs>? (4) How to handle embedded raster images (<image>)? (5) How to handle transforms on elements? (6) Strategy for graceful degradation — unsupported elements should be preserved in the DOM for round-trip fidelity but clearly marked as non-editable. Document findings and decisions.
title: S10-05: RESEARCH — SVG import fidelity and unsupported element handling

description: Implement distribute-horizontal and distribute-vertical for 3+ selected elements. Calculates even spacing between elements. Add 2 buttons next to the align UI in PropertiesPanel. Uses CompoundCommand. Key files: align.ts, PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Distribute commands (horizontal & vertical)

description: Implement SVG import. Open a native file dialog via Tauri's dialog API to select an SVG file. Read the file contents via Tauri's fs API. Parse the SVG string into DOM elements and load them into the document model. Preserve element structure, attributes, and hierarchy. Map top-level <g> elements to layers. Handle basic SVG elements: <line>, <rect>, <ellipse>, <circle>, <path>, <text>, <g>. Unsupported elements are preserved as-is in the DOM but may not be fully editable. For dev mode without Tauri, support file input via browser file picker. This replaces the current document. Include tests for SVG parsing.
title: S10-04: SVG import — open SVG file via Tauri file dialog, parse into document model

description: In selectTool.ts, getPositionAttrs (lines 217-231) and moveElement (lines 233-262) handle line, rect, ellipse, circle, and text but NOT path. When a user draws a Bezier path with the pen tool and tries to move it with the select tool, moveElement does nothing — the path is selected but immovable. Similarly, scaleElement and getAllGeomAttrs don't handle path. Also, paste offset in EditorContext.tsx (lines 58-71) and arrow-key nudge (lines 156-183) skip path elements. Fix: For path elements, apply movement via a translate() transform, or rewrite the d attribute coordinates. Also handle <g> (group) elements. Key files: src/tools/selectTool.ts, src/model/EditorContext.tsx, src/model/align.ts.
title: Path elements cannot be moved or scaled with select tool

description: In grid.ts lines 87 and 102, Math.abs(x % settings.majorSpacing) < 0.01 is used to detect major grid lines. This fails for non-binary-representable spacings. Example: minorSpacing=0.3, majorSpacing=3 — after 10 additions of 0.3, accumulated value is 2.9999999999999996, and 2.999... % 3.0 = 2.999..., which is NOT < 0.01. Major grid lines render as minor lines. Fix: Use iteration counting instead: const isMajor = (i % (majorSpacing / minorSpacing) === 0), or compute isMajor via Math.round(x / majorSpacing) * majorSpacing comparison. Key files: src/model/grid.ts.
title: Grid major line detection fails with floating-point modulo

description: Replace the horizontal toolbar with a 32-40px vertical strip on the far left side of the app. Square icon buttons stacked vertically with active state highlight. Create new ToolStrip.tsx component. Update App.tsx layout to use vertical strip instead of horizontal toolbar. Key files: new ToolStrip.tsx, App.tsx.
title: Vertical tool strip

description: Implement a default style system. When the user changes the stroke or fill color of an element, that color becomes the new default for subsequently created elements. Store defaults as module-level state: { stroke: string, fill: string, strokeWidth: number }. Drawing tools (line, rect, ellipse, pen) apply these defaults to newly created elements. Initial defaults: stroke='#000000', fill='none', strokeWidth=1. When no element is selected and the user picks a color, it updates the default style directly. Include tests.
title: S6-06: Default style — new objects inherit last-used stroke/fill

description: PropertyInput in PropertiesPanel.tsx calls onChange on every keystroke, which creates and executes a ModifyAttributeCommand. Typing '123' into a width field pushes 3 commands to the undo stack. Ctrl+Z undoes one character at a time (123 -> 12 -> 1 -> original). This also causes layout thrashing as the SVG element resizes per keystroke. Fix: Only call onChange on blur or Enter, not on every keystroke. Use localValue for live preview and commit the final value as a single command. Key files: src/components/PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Property inputs fire undo commands on every keystroke

description: During element drag, show temporary alignment lines when element edges or centers align with other elements. Snap to guides within 2mm tolerance. Render guide lines as SVG lines in the overlay group with data-role=overlay. Create new smartGuides.ts module that computes alignment candidates from all non-dragged elements. Key files: new smartGuides.ts, selectTool.ts.
title: Smart guides (alignment snapping)

description: Implement the status bar at the bottom of the app. It should display: (1) Cursor position in mm (e.g., 'X: 105.0 mm  Y: 148.5 mm') updated in real-time as the mouse moves over the canvas, (2) Current zoom level as a percentage (e.g., '100%'). Use monospace font for coordinate/dimension readouts. The status bar should also show selection info when objects are selected (placeholder for now). Wire up mouse move events on the canvas to update the coordinate display using the screenToDoc conversion from S1-01.
title: S1-04: Status bar — cursor position in mm and zoom level display

description: Evaluate PDF export approaches for SVG: svg2pdf.js (pure JS, client-side), Tauri backend with rsvg-convert, pdf-lib with manual rendering. Test fidelity with arrow markers, text elements, dash patterns. Output a decision document at docs/research/pdf-export.md with pros/cons and recommendation. Key consideration: vectorfeld is a Vite+React app (not Tauri), so pure JS solutions are preferred.
title: PDF export research

description: Add a <defs> element to SVG initialization (before layers). Add getDefs() method to DocumentModel in document.ts. Preserve <defs> content during SVG export in fileio.ts. Import <defs> content when opening SVG files. This is the foundation for markers, gradients, and patterns. Key files: Canvas.tsx, document.ts, fileio.ts.
title: SVG <defs> infrastructure

description: Wire up the color picker to the fill attribute of selected elements. In the properties panel, show a 'Fill' section with a color swatch displaying the current fill color. Clicking the swatch opens the color picker. Selecting a color (from palette, hex, or RGB) applies it to all selected elements' fill attribute via ModifyAttributeCommand (undoable). Support 'none' for no fill (transparent). When multiple elements are selected with different fills, show a mixed-state indicator. Include tests.
title: S6-04: Apply fill color to selection

description: Shift should constrain to 45/90 degree angles for line, rectangle, ellipse tools. Shift+ellipse for circle constraint partially works. Alt key for draw-from-center.
title: Shift-key angle snapping for drawing tools

description: Implement the ellipse drawing tool. Workflow: (1) select ellipse tool (shortcut: 'e'), (2) click on canvas to set center point, (3) drag — a preview ellipse follows with radii determined by cursor distance from center, (4) release mouse to commit. The ellipse is created as an SVG <ellipse> element via AddElementCommand. Attributes: cx, cy (center), rx, ry (radii) in document coordinates. Holding Shift constrains to a circle (rx = ry = distance from center to cursor). Apply current default stroke and fill styles. Include tests.
title: S3-04: Ellipse tool — click-drag from center, shift-constrain to circle, creates <ellipse>

description: Implement the rectangle drawing tool. Workflow: (1) select rectangle tool (shortcut: 'r'), (2) click and drag on canvas — a preview rectangle follows from click point to cursor, (3) release mouse to commit. The rectangle is created as an SVG <rect> element via AddElementCommand. Attributes: x, y (top-left corner), width, height in document coordinates (mm). Holding Shift constrains the rectangle to a square (width = height = min(width, height)). The rectangle should handle drag in any direction (user can drag up-left from the start point). Apply current default stroke and fill styles. Include tests.
title: S3-03: Rectangle tool — click-drag, shift-constrain to square, creates <rect>

description: The tool registry (registry.ts) has no onDeactivate callback. When switching tools mid-operation: (1) Line/rect/ellipse tools leave orphaned preview elements in SVG DOM. (2) Text tool leaves a running setInterval for caret blink (memory leak). (3) Pen tool leaves multiple preview elements (path, anchors, handles). (4) Direct select tool leaves anchor/handle visuals. (5) Select tool leaves a marquee rect mid-drag. Fix: Add optional onDeactivate to ToolConfig interface. Call it in setActiveTool before switching. Each tool implements cleanup in onDeactivate. Key files: src/tools/registry.ts, all tool files.
title: Add tool deactivation hook to prevent state leaks

close_reason: Added 5px tolerance buffer for thin elements in hit testing (select + eraser)
description: Hit-testing on near-zero-width elements is too strict. Fix: add stroke-width-aware tolerance buffer (minimum ~5px hit area). Related to resize handles issue.
title: Thin/vertical lines hard to select

description: Three new tool features have zero test coverage: (1) Marquee selection in selectTool.ts — AABB intersection logic, locked/hidden layer filtering, small-marquee deselect, cleanup of marquee rect. (2) snapLineAngle in lineTool.ts — angle snapping at 0/45/90/135/180/225/270/315 degrees, shift=false passthrough, zero-length guard. (3) Eyedropper tool — sampling stroke/fill/strokeWidth from clicked element, click on empty area, layer traversal. Key files: new src/tools/selectTool.test.ts, src/tools/lineTool.test.ts, src/tools/eyedropperTool.test.ts.
title: Add tests for marquee selection, shift-snap, eyedropper

description: Implement the artboard/document setup. Create a dialog or panel where the user can set document dimensions (width × height) in mm or cm. Provide presets for common sizes (A4: 210×297mm, A3: 297×420mm, Letter: 215.9×279.4mm, custom). Orientation toggle (portrait/landscape) swaps width and height. When dimensions change, update the SVG viewBox and root element width/height attributes. Store document metadata (dimensions, units) in the SVG <metadata> element or namespaced attributes. Default to A4 portrait on new document.
title: S1-05: Artboard setup — document dimensions dialog, updates viewBox

description: computeSmartGuides() calls collectCandidates() on every mousemove during drag, which iterates all elements in all layers and calls getBBox() on each. Non-dragged elements don't move during a drag, so their alignment edges are stable. With 200+ elements, this is 1200+ getBBox() calls per frame. Fix: Collect and cache candidates once when the drag starts (in selectTool onMouseDown), pass the cached candidates to computeSmartGuides on each mousemove. Key files: src/model/smartGuides.ts, src/tools/selectTool.ts.
title: Smart guides: cache candidates at drag-start instead of per-frame

description: Add marker-start and marker-end dropdown selectors in PropertiesPanel for line and path elements. Options: none, triangle, open, reverse, circle. Sets marker-start='url(#marker-id)' and marker-end='url(#marker-id)' via ModifyAttributeCommand. Calls ensureMarkerDef() to create the marker definition if needed. Key files: PropertiesPanel.tsx, markers.ts.
title: Arrow marker UI (start/end dropdowns)

description: At least 8 bare catch blocks (catch { /* skip */ }) across the codebase swallow ALL exceptions, not just the expected getBBox DOMException. Locations: selection.ts:130, selection.ts:193, selectTool.ts:45, selectTool.ts:194, selectTool.ts:586, smartGuides.ts:46, align.ts:44, eyedropperTool.ts:30. These hide real bugs — if code after getBBox() throws (e.g., transformedAABB regex parsing), the error is silently eaten. Fix: Either catch only DOMException, or at minimum log unexpected errors. Key files: multiple.
title: Replace bare catch blocks with specific error handling

close_reason: EditorContext refactoring deferred; functional bugs all fixed
description: EditorContext.tsx is 260+ lines with a single useEffect containing a massive keyboard handler that implements: copy, cut, paste, duplicate, group, ungroup, undo, redo, nudge, delete, grid toggle, and 4 z-order operations. Each new shortcut makes it worse. Fix: Extract keyboard bindings into a separate keyboardCommands.ts module that maps key combos to named action functions. Move clipboard, group/ungroup, and z-order logic into dedicated model modules. Key files: src/model/EditorContext.tsx, new src/model/keyboardCommands.ts.
title: Extract keyboard commands from EditorContext god-file

description: Create the layers panel component (left side of app, collapsible). It displays a list of layers in the document. Each layer corresponds to a top-level <g> element with a data-layer-name attribute. The panel shows: layer name (editable on double-click), a colored indicator. Actions: 'Add layer' button creates a new <g> element via AddElementCommand. 'Delete layer' button removes the selected layer and all its children (with confirmation if non-empty). The default document starts with one layer named 'Layer 1'. Clicking a layer makes it the active layer. Include tests.
title: S7-01: Layers panel UI — list of layers, add/delete layer

close_reason: Added lock aspect ratio toggle for Size inputs (rect W/H, ellipse RX/RY). Added letter-spacing (Lsp) input to text Font section. All 114 tests passing.
description: Add font-size and letter-spacing numeric inputs to the properties panel for text elements. Font size: numeric input in points (pt) or mm. Common sizes as presets (8, 10, 12, 14, 18, 24, 36, 48, 72). Changing font size updates the selected text element's font-size attribute via ModifyAttributeCommand. Letter spacing: numeric input in mm. Updates letter-spacing attribute. Both inputs show current values when a text element is selected. When multiple text elements are selected with differing values, show a placeholder. Include tests.
title: S9-04: Font size and letter-spacing inputs in properties panel

description: Two code paths move element positions without updating the rotation center in the transform attribute: (1) Arrow-key nudge in EditorContext.tsx lines 156-183 modifies x/y/cx/cy but not the rotation center in transform='rotate(angle, cx, cy)'. (2) Paste in EditorContext.tsx lines 58-71 offsets positions by 5mm but leaves the rotation center unchanged. Both cause the rotation center to become detached from the element, making the element 'orbit' its old position on subsequent rotations. Compare with moveElement() in selectTool.ts and applyDelta() in align.ts which correctly update rotation centers. Fix: After modifying position attributes, also parse and update the rotation center in the transform attribute. Key files: src/model/EditorContext.tsx.
title: Nudge and paste don't update rotation center in transforms

description: Create the document model: a thin wrapper around the SVG DOM that provides a clean API for manipulating the document. The wrapper should support: (1) addElement(parentId, element) — add an SVG element to a parent group/layer, (2) removeElement(id) — remove an element by ID, (3) modifyAttribute(id, attr, value) — change an attribute on an element, (4) getElement(id) — retrieve an element, (5) serialize() — export the full SVG DOM as an SVG string. All elements get unique IDs assigned on creation. The document model is the single source of truth — all mutations go through it. Layer support: top-level <g> elements with data-layer-name attributes represent layers. Include tests for all CRUD operations and serialization.
title: S2-01: Document model — thin SVG DOM wrapper with element add/remove/modify

description: In LayersPanel.tsx, all layer operations (addLayer, deleteLayer, moveLayerUp, moveLayerDown, toggleVisibility, toggleLock) directly mutate the SVG DOM without going through CommandHistory. Accidentally deleting a layer with 50 elements is permanent data loss — Ctrl+Z does nothing. Fix: Create AddLayerCommand, RemoveLayerCommand, and ReorderLayerCommand classes. Visibility and lock toggling could use ModifyAttributeCommand. Key files: src/components/LayersPanel.tsx, src/model/commands.ts.
title: Layer add/delete/reorder/visibility/lock bypass command history

description: Render a grid overlay with 10mm major lines and 5mm minor lines in the overlay group of the canvas. Toggle visibility with Ctrl+' keyboard shortcut. Create a new grid.ts state module following the pub-sub pattern (getGridSettings, setGridSettings, subscribeGrid). Grid renders as SVG lines with data-role=overlay so they are stripped on export. Key files: new grid.ts, Canvas.tsx, EditorContext.tsx.
title: Grid display overlay

close_reason: Implemented scale-drag via handles. Corner handles scale both axes, edge handles scale one axis. Shift constrains proportions. Supports rect, ellipse, circle, line, text elements. Fully undoable via reset-then-execute pattern. All 110 tests passing.
description: Implement scaling via dragging scale handles. Dragging a corner handle scales the selected object(s) from the opposite corner as the anchor point. The scale factor is computed from the ratio of new size to original size. Holding Shift constrains proportions (aspect ratio preserved). Edge midpoint handles scale in one axis only. During drag, show a real-time preview of the scaled object(s). On mouse release, create a ScaleCommand (undoable) that modifies the element's transform attribute or geometry attributes. Include tests for scale math and proportion constraint.
title: S5-02: Scale — drag corner handle, shift-constrain proportions

close_reason: Performance acceptable after smart guide caching; full incremental overlay deferred
description: refreshOverlay() tears down and rebuilds the entire selection overlay (8 handle rects + 4 rotation zones + N selection boxes) on every mousemove during drag operations. That's 60 full rebuilds per second. Fix: During active drag, update existing handle element positions via setAttribute instead of removing and recreating all elements. Or skip overlay refresh entirely during drag (most editors hide handles while dragging). Key files: src/model/selection.ts.
title: Selection overlay: incremental update instead of full rebuild

close_reason: Rotation handle added (circle above top-center, connected by line, grab cursor, single-selection only). Font family dropdown with 11 safe fonts added to properties panel for text elements. 3 new tests, all 114 passing.
description: Add a font family selector to the properties panel for text elements. For MVP, use a hardcoded list of safe/common fonts that are widely available: sans-serif (Arial/Helvetica), serif (Times New Roman/Georgia), monospace (Courier New/Consolas), and a few others (Verdana, Trebuchet MS, etc.). Render the dropdown with each font name displayed in that font for preview. Selecting a font updates the selected text element's font-family attribute via ModifyAttributeCommand (undoable). System font enumeration via Tauri is deferred to research issue R-02. Include tests.
title: S9-03: Font family dropdown — enumerate system fonts or hardcoded safe list

description: After rotating an element, show an oriented bounding box (OBB) that follows the rotation rather than an axis-aligned bounding box (AABB). Advanced selection rendering.
title: Oriented bounding box for rotated elements

description: Draw a rectangle on canvas to select multiple elements. Standard vector editor feature. User acknowledged not MVP scope.
title: Marquee / drag selection

close_reason: Eraser shows red outline highlight on hover before erasing
description: User wants elements to highlight on hover before erasing. Currently only shows not-allowed cursor. Fix: add red outline on elements under cursor. File: eraserTool.ts
title: Eraser tool needs hover highlight feedback

close_reason: Implemented text tool (shortcut T). Click to place, type to create SVG text element with blinking caret. Enter/Escape commits text. Keyboard capture prevents tool shortcuts during typing. Fully undoable. 14 new tests, all 104 tests passing.
description: Implement the point text tool (shortcut: 't'). Workflow: (1) Click on the canvas to place a text insertion point. (2) A text cursor (blinking caret) appears at the clicked position. (3) The user types — characters are rendered as an SVG <text> element at that position. (4) Clicking elsewhere or pressing Escape exits text editing mode and commits the text element via AddElementCommand. The text element uses the current default font family, size, and color. Position the <text> element at the click point's document coordinates. The x,y attributes set the text baseline position. Include tests.
title: S9-01: Point text tool — click to place, enter edit mode, type to create <text>

description: In PropertiesPanel.tsx, when lock-aspect is enabled and the user changes width, applyAttr is called twice separately: once for height, once for width. Each creates an independent ModifyAttributeCommand via history.execute(). Ctrl+Z only undoes the width change, leaving height at the new value — corrupting the aspect ratio. Fix: Use a CompoundCommand that batches both width and height changes into a single undo entry. Key files: src/components/PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Aspect-ratio lock creates two separate undo entries

description: Implement the selection tool (shortcut: 'v'). Click on an SVG element to select it. Click on empty canvas to deselect all. Selection state is managed globally. When an element is selected, display a visual bounding box around it (a dashed rectangle overlay showing the element's bounds). The bounding box is rendered as a non-document SVG overlay (not part of the document model). Use element.getBBox() to compute bounds. Selection should work with all element types: <line>, <rect>, <ellipse>, <path>, <text>, <g>. Include tests for hit detection and selection state management.
title: S4-01: Selection tool — click to select, click empty to deselect, visual bounding box

description: Add visibility and lock toggles to each layer in the layers panel. Visibility toggle: an eye icon that toggles the layer's <g> element display attribute between 'inline' and 'none'. Hidden layers are not rendered on the canvas. Lock toggle: a lock icon that marks the layer as locked. Elements on locked layers cannot be selected, moved, or modified. Locked layers' elements are excluded from hit testing. Both toggles are visual-only (do not create undo commands — they are view state, not document state). Include tests.
title: S7-02: Layer visibility toggle and lock toggle

close_reason: Research complete: No official Tauri font plugin. Best approach: Rust Tauri command using font-kit crate (SystemSource API) for cross-platform font enumeration (DirectWrite/CoreText/Fontconfig). Returns structured JSON. Alternative: fc-list via shell (Linux-only). Current hardcoded 11-font list is fine for MVP.
description: Research issue: investigate how to enumerate system fonts from within a Tauri v2 application. Options to evaluate: (1) Tauri plugin for font enumeration (does one exist?). (2) Run 'fc-list' via Tauri backend shell command and parse output. (3) Use a font-manager npm package that works in webview. (4) Hardcoded safe list for MVP (current approach in S9-03). Document: which approach gives the best UX, what are the performance implications, does it work on both Linux and Windows. Decide and document recommended approach for post-MVP font enumeration.
title: S11-03: RESEARCH — Font enumeration strategy for Tauri/webview

description: Support <linearGradient> elements in <defs>. Add a fill-type selector in PropertiesPanel (Solid / Linear Gradient / None). When Linear Gradient selected, show start color picker, end color picker, and angle control. Create gradient definition in <defs> and set fill='url(#gradient-id)' on element. Create new gradients.ts module for gradient management. Key files: new gradients.ts, PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Linear gradient fill

description: Install and configure Vitest as the test runner. Add vitest.config.ts with jsdom environment for DOM/SVG testing. Create a basic smoke test that verifies the app shell renders. Configure npm scripts: 'npm test' and 'npm run test:watch'. Vitest is chosen because it is Vite-native, fast, and TypeScript-first.
title: S0-02: Vitest setup with basic smoke test

description: In selectTool.ts line 506, when scaling elements with zero-dimension bounding boxes (e.g., vertical lines have width=0, horizontal lines have height=0), the scale factor becomes Infinity (newWidth / origBBox.width = 0.1 / 0 = Infinity). This writes 'Infinity' or 'NaN' to DOM attributes, permanently corrupting the element's geometry. Fix: Guard against zero-dimension bounding boxes before computing scale ratios. If origBBox.width === 0 and axes.scaleX, skip X scaling. Key files: src/tools/selectTool.ts.
title: SHOWSTOPPER: Scale division by zero corrupts element geometry

close_reason: Rotation via dragging rotation handle works. Angle computed from atan2 vectors relative to bbox center. Shift constrains to 15-degree increments. Sets transform=rotate(angle, cx, cy). Fully undoable. All 114 tests passing.
description: Implement rotation via dragging the rotation handle. Rotation is around the center of the selected element's bounding box. The rotation angle is computed from the angle between the vector from center to the initial mouse position and the vector from center to the current mouse position. Holding Shift constrains rotation to 15° increments (snaps to nearest 15°). During drag, show a real-time preview. On mouse release, create a RotateCommand (undoable) that sets or modifies the element's transform='rotate(angle, cx, cy)' attribute. Include tests for rotation math and snapping.
title: S5-05: Rotate — drag rotation handle, shift-constrain to 15° increments

description: Create the properties panel component (right side of app, collapsible). The panel is context-sensitive: it shows different content based on what is selected. When nothing is selected, show document properties (artboard dimensions). When an element is selected, show transform properties (X, Y, W, H, rotation) and style properties (stroke, fill). When text is selected, additionally show font properties. The panel should have sections with headers that can be collapsed. Use Tailwind CSS, small dense controls per design principles. This is the shell — specific property inputs are added in subsequent issues.
title: S6-01: Properties panel shell — context-sensitive, shows for selected object

description: Extend the eraser tool: when the user clicks and drags, all elements the cursor passes over are deleted. Collect elements hit during the drag and delete them all on mouse release as a single compound command (undoable — one undo restores everything erased in that drag). Use continuous hit testing along the drag path (sample at regular intervals, not just mouse move events, to avoid gaps). Elements on locked layers are skipped. Include tests.
title: S10-02: Eraser tool — drag to delete objects the cursor passes over

description: Create the toolbar component (horizontal bar at top of app). It reads all registered tools from the tool registry and renders a button for each. Each button shows: (1) the tool's icon, (2) a tooltip with the tool name and keyboard shortcut (e.g., 'Line (L)'). The active tool's button is visually highlighted (accent color background or border). Clicking a button switches the active tool. Buttons should be small, sharp, tight spacing per the design principles. Style with Tailwind CSS — grays for chrome, one accent color for active state.
title: S3-05: Toolbar UI — tool buttons with active state indication

description: Create a new defaultStyle.ts module following the pub-sub pattern from selection.ts. Track last-used stroke, fill, and strokeWidth. Drawing tools should read defaults from this module instead of hardcoding #000000. PropertiesPanel updates defaults whenever the user changes style properties on a selected element. Key files: new defaultStyle.ts, all drawing tools (lineTool.ts, rectTool.ts, ellipseTool.ts, pathTool.ts), PropertiesPanel.tsx. Closes S6-06.
title: Default style (last-used stroke/fill inheritance)

description: Implement active layer selection. The currently active layer is highlighted in the layers panel. When drawing tools create new elements, the elements are appended to the active layer's <g> element (not the root SVG). The active layer defaults to the first layer. Clicking a layer in the layers panel makes it the active layer. If the active layer is hidden or locked, show a warning/indicator and either auto-switch to the next available layer or prevent drawing. Include tests.
title: S7-04: Active layer — new objects are created in the active layer

description: Create preset SVG markers (triangle/filled arrow, open arrow, reverse arrow, circle/dot) as <marker> elements in <defs>. Create new markers.ts module with marker templates and an ensureMarkerDef(svgRoot, markerType) function that lazily creates marker definitions. Markers should have proper viewBox, refX/refY, and orient=auto. Key files: new markers.ts.
title: Arrow marker definitions

description: In fileio.ts, importSvg wraps a file input in a Promise with only resolve (no reject). If the user cancels the file dialog, onchange never fires and the promise hangs forever. If FileReader encounters an error (reader.onerror), the promise also hangs. If a non-SVG file is selected, DOMParser produces an error document that is silently processed, importing nothing with no feedback. Fix: Add a cancel handler (input.addEventListener('cancel', resolve)), add reader.onerror = reject, validate the parsed SVG document before processing. Key files: src/model/fileio.ts.
title: importSvg promise hangs on user cancel or file error

close_reason: Replaced top-center rotation handle with Illustrator-style invisible corner rotation zones
description: User prefers Illustrator-style rotation: hover near bounding box corners shows rotation cursor, drag to rotate. Instead of current dedicated rotation handle at top-center. File: selectTool.ts, selection.ts
title: Corner rotation handles (Illustrator-style)

close_reason: Polling acceptable for MVP; pub-sub refactor deferred
description: LayersPanel.tsx uses setInterval(refreshLayers, 500) to poll for layer changes instead of subscribing to DOM mutations. This causes: (1) Unnecessary re-renders every 500ms even when nothing changed. (2) Up to 500ms latency for layer changes to appear. (3) Inconsistency with all other modules that use pub-sub. Fix: Add a document change notification to the document model (or use MutationObserver on the SVG element) so the layers panel updates immediately when layers change. Key files: src/components/LayersPanel.tsx.
title: LayersPanel: replace 500ms polling with pub-sub

description: In align.ts, applyDelta (lines 169-196) handles line, rect, text, ellipse, circle but not path, polygon, polyline, or g (group). When a selection includes a path element and the user clicks 'Align Left', the path doesn't move — applyDelta returns an empty array. Fix: For path elements, apply translation via modifying the transform attribute (add/update translate()). Same approach for group elements. Key files: src/model/align.ts.
title: Path elements cannot participate in align/distribute

description: In selectTool.ts lines 355-359, when starting a rotation drag, the rotation center is computed from unionBBox (AABB center). For asymmetric shapes (e.g., a line), the AABB center shifts as the element rotates. Each rotation operation picks a new center based on the current AABB, causing cumulative drift. After multiple rotations, the element orbits around a moving point. Fix: Use the center from the existing rotation transform if one exists (parse the cx, cy from rotate(angle, cx, cy)). Only compute from bbox for the first rotation. Key files: src/tools/selectTool.ts.
title: Rotation center drifts on successive rotations of asymmetric shapes

close_reason: Added rotation angle input (Rot) to Transform section in properties panel. Shows current rotation extracted from transform attribute, allows typing exact angle. Undoable. All 114 tests passing.
description: Add a rotation angle input field in the properties panel. When an element is selected, show the current rotation angle in degrees. The user can type an exact angle (e.g., '45') and the element rotates to that absolute angle around its bounding box center. Each change creates a RotateCommand (undoable). If the element already has a rotation transform, the displayed angle reflects the current rotation. Include tests.
title: S5-06: Rotate — numeric input for exact angle

description: Wire up keyboard shortcuts for undo/redo. Ctrl+Z triggers undo, Ctrl+Shift+Z triggers redo. Register these as global keyboard event handlers. They should: (1) call commandHistory.undo()/redo(), (2) update the canvas to reflect the reverted/reapplied state, (3) be disabled (no-op) when canUndo/canRedo is false. Ensure the shortcuts don't conflict with text editing (when a text input is focused, defer to browser defaults). Include tests verifying the keybinding wiring.
title: S2-03: Undo/redo keybindings — Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Shift+Z wired to command history

description: Support <radialGradient> elements in <defs>. Add 'Radial Gradient' option to fill-type selector. Show center position and radius controls. Shares gradient infrastructure from gradients.ts. Key files: gradients.ts, PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Radial gradient fill

description: Transform the visual identity from prototype to app with Illustrator-style layout. This is the parent epic. Sub-issues: vertical tool strip, menu bar, collapsible panels, tool icons.
title: Illustrator CS4/5/6 style UI layout

description: Critical integration flows lack tests: (1) Gradient creation then parseGradientColors roundtrip — do colors survive? (2) Marker ensureMarkerDef idempotency — calling twice doesn't create duplicates. (3) Export SVG strips overlay/preview/grid/guide elements but preserves defs. (4) Import SVG preserves gradient and marker definitions from imported file. (5) Export-then-import roundtrip preserves element attributes including stroke-dasharray, opacity, marker references. (6) getDefs returns valid defs element, doesn't duplicate on repeated calls. Key files: src/model/fileio.ts, src/model/gradients.ts, src/model/markers.ts, src/model/document.ts.
title: Add integration tests for export/import and defs roundtrip

description: In selection.ts, when a single rotated element is selected, scale handles are positioned on the OBB (local bbox + element transform). But in selectTool.ts, the scale anchor is computed from unionBBox which returns the AABB (axis-aligned bounding box). The user sees handles at OBB corners but the scale computation uses AABB corners — different points. Dragging a rotated element's apparent NW handle scales from the AABB's SE corner, not the OBB's SE corner. Fix: When scaling a single rotated element, compute the anchor from the local bbox (matching the OBB handles), not from the AABB. Key files: src/tools/selectTool.ts, src/model/selection.ts.
title: OBB scale handles mismatch AABB anchor computation

description: Wire up the color picker to the stroke attribute of selected elements. In the properties panel, show a 'Stroke' section with a color swatch displaying the current stroke color. Clicking the swatch opens the color picker. Selecting a color applies it to all selected elements' stroke attribute via ModifyAttributeCommand (undoable). When multiple elements are selected with different strokes, show a mixed-state indicator. Default stroke for new elements is black (#000000). Include tests.
title: S6-05: Apply stroke color to selection

close_reason: Open now clears existing layers before importing; preserves layer structure from saved files
description: User reports that opening a saved document creates elements on top of existing canvas rather than clearing and replacing. File open handler likely appends SVG elements instead of clearing first.
title: Open file overlays on existing canvas instead of replacing

description: Implement the command pattern for undo/redo. Define a Command interface with: execute(), undo(), and a human-readable description. Create the CommandHistory class that maintains: (1) an undo stack of executed commands, (2) a redo stack cleared on new command execution. Methods: execute(cmd) — runs cmd.execute() and pushes to undo stack, undo() — pops from undo stack, calls cmd.undo(), pushes to redo stack, redo() — pops from redo stack, calls cmd.execute(), pushes to undo stack, canUndo/canRedo getters. Implement initial concrete commands: AddElementCommand, RemoveElementCommand, ModifyAttributeCommand — each wrapping the corresponding document model operation and its inverse. Include thorough tests for execute/undo/redo sequences.
title: S2-02: Command history — base command interface (execute/undo) and history stack

description: Integrate the PDF export library chosen in the research phase. Add 'Export PDF' button in toolbar or File menu. Clean the SVG (strip overlays, normalize viewBox), convert to PDF, trigger browser download. Handle edge cases: markers, text, dashes, gradients. Key files: fileio.ts, Toolbar.tsx or MenuBar.tsx, App.tsx, package.json.
title: PDF export implementation

description: Add up/down arrow buttons per layer row in LayersPanel.tsx. Use insertBefore on SVG DOM to reorder <g> elements. This allows users to change the visual stacking order of layers. Key files: LayersPanel.tsx. Closes S7-03 from the original MVP plan.
title: Layer reordering (up/down buttons)

description: In gradients.ts, both createLinearGradient and createRadialGradient use gradientUnits='objectBoundingBox'. For line elements with zero width or height (horizontal/vertical lines), the objectBoundingBox coordinate system becomes degenerate (division by zero in one axis). The gradient will not render — the line appears with no stroke or solid color. Fix: Either use gradientUnits='userSpaceOnUse' with document-space coordinates, or disable gradient fills for line elements in the Properties Panel UI. Key files: src/model/gradients.ts, src/components/PropertiesPanel.tsx.
title: Gradient fills break on line elements (zero-dimension bbox)

description: Implement move by dragging. When one or more objects are selected and the user clicks and drags on a selected element, all selected elements move with the cursor. Movement is computed as the delta between current and previous mouse position in document coordinates. On mouse release, a MoveElementCommand is created and pushed to the command history (making the move undoable). The command stores the element IDs and the total delta. For groups of selected elements, a single compound command moves all of them. Preview during drag updates element positions in real-time. Include tests.
title: S4-04: Move — drag selected objects, creates MoveElement command

description: Implement the eraser tool (shortcut: 'x'). When the eraser tool is active and the user clicks on a document element, that element is deleted via RemoveElementCommand (undoable). Hit detection works like the selection tool — use the click point to find the topmost element under the cursor. Elements on locked layers cannot be erased. The cursor should change to an eraser icon. Include tests.
title: S10-01: Eraser tool — click on object to delete it

description: The transformedAABB function (parse rotate() from transform, rotate corners, compute AABB) is copy-pasted in 4 files: selection.ts:84, selectTool.ts:149, smartGuides.ts:52, align.ts:14. All only handle rotate() and silently ignore translate/scale/matrix transforms. A bug fix in one copy doesn't propagate to others. Fix: Extract to a shared src/model/geometry.ts module. Consider using SVGGraphicsElement.getCTM() for proper transform handling of all transform types. Also extract the shared hitTest function (duplicated in selectTool, eyedropperTool, eraserTool). Key files: src/model/geometry.ts (new), selection.ts, selectTool.ts, smartGuides.ts, align.ts.
title: Extract shared transformedAABB utility (deduplicate 4 copies)
